lesson: A BEATER FAULT with a freely-spinning beater, healthy windings and an erratic Hall sensor is a position-sensor fault. The cap-start swap is the trap for techs trained on older machines.
symptoms: Beater makes a grinding attempt then stops.

tests.error-log: BEATER POSITION FAULT — controller can't read rotor position. Logged since Wednesday.
tests.inspect-beater: Beater spins freely by hand. No ice, no seizure, nothing jammed.
tests.continuity-test: Motor windings healthy. Hall effect position sensor reads erratic — signal wire has a hairline break near the connector.

flavour: Couldn't tell where it was. The motor agreed.
authenticityNote: Modern brushless beater drives rely on Hall effect sensors for commutation; a broken sensor wire presents as a BEATER FAULT with a completely healthy motor. The classic cap-start fault is the obvious trap for techs who learned on older machines.
